mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, generating sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter through higher revenue growth.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive quarterly returns (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.05 over 5-year walk-forward).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves short-term corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases (t-stat < 1.5 over 10-year sample).

mechanism_narrative: Low policy rates combined with stable unemployment create a favorable risk-free rate environment that supports equity valuations, producing positive forward returns when monetary policy remains accommodative despite labor market softness.
failure_condition: The interaction term between low rates and stable unemployment yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as discount rates rise and demand slows.
failure_condition: Months with CPI inflation exceeding 0.4% month-over-month show positive 20-day equity returns in > 60% of cases over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with quarterly forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDP growth combined with declining industrial production signals a synchronized slowdown in aggregate demand and manufacturing output, triggering negative short-term equity returns as recession fears intensify.
failure_condition: The joint signal of sub-1.5% GDP growth and falling industrial production fails to predict negative 5-day returns (hit rate < 45% over 10-year out-of-sample testing).
